settings.text: One team and one contract, from permits to punch list. If it's on this page, we've built it many times before.

settings.title: Kitchen & bath remodels
settings.description: Full gut-and-rebuild or a careful refresh — cabinetry, tile, plumbing, and electrical handled by one crew, with the same finish standard either way.

settings.title: Home additions
settings.description: Second stories, rear extensions, and in-law suites. Engineered, permitted, and tied into the original structure so it reads as one house.

settings.title: Whole-home renovations
settings.description: Down-to-the-studs rebuilds of houses worth saving — new systems, new layouts, original character kept where it counts.

settings.title: Decks & outdoor structures
settings.description: Cedar decks, pergolas, and covered porches built to the same code and the same standard as everything else we do.

settings.title: Light commercial fit-outs
settings.description: Cafés, shops, and small offices. Tight timelines are the whole job here — we plan backwards from your opening day.

settings.text: Our own carpenters do the core work. The plumbers and electricians we bring in have been with us for over a decade. Either way, you have one contract, one point of contact, and one company responsible for the result.

settings.features:
+ Dedicated project manager on every job
+ Daily site cleanup, no exceptions
+ Fixed written quotes before work starts

settings.question: Who handles the permits?
settings.answer: We do — filing, fees, inspections, all of it. Permit costs appear as a line item in your quote, at cost. If a project doesn't need a permit, we'll tell you that too.

settings.question: Can we live in the house during the work?
settings.answer: Usually, yes. We seal the work zone with dust barriers, keep one bathroom functional at all times on bath projects, and set up a temporary kitchen corner on kitchen jobs. Whole-home renovations are the exception — we'll be honest about when moving out saves you money and misery.

settings.question: What happens if we change our minds mid-project?
settings.answer: Changes get a written change order with a price and a schedule impact before anything happens. You sign it or you don't. No verbal add-ons, no surprise invoices at the end.

settings.question: Do you use subcontractors?
settings.answer: For plumbing, electrical, and HVAC — yes, licensed specialists we've worked with for years. Carpentry, tile, and finish work stay in-house. Every sub on your job is covered by our insurance requirements and supervised by our site superintendent.

settings.question: How does the payment schedule work?
settings.answer: A deposit to secure your slot, then progress payments tied to completed milestones — never to dates. The final payment isn't due until after the walkthrough, when the punch list is cleared. You never pay for work that hasn't happened.

settings.title: Not sure where your project fits?
settings.text:
hello@example.com

Send a few photos and a rough idea. We'll tell you honestly whether it's our kind of job — and roughly what it runs.
Get a fixed price for your project.
Free site visit, written quote within five days.
